articles.title: The Week in Breakingviews: Causes of correlation
articles.description: Despite renewed hostilities in ⁠the Strait ​of Hormuz, investors remain ​obsessed with artificial intelligence. This week’s update ​argues there will ​be few places to ‌hide ⁠when the AI trade goes into reverse. Plus: looking ​for ​Japan’s ⁠missing migrants, and a gamble ​on Tobin ​taxes.
articles.content: LONDON, July 12 (Reuters Breakingviews) - Welcome back! The ceasefire in the Strait of Hormuz ended, but financial markets shrugged. Investors are more focused on artificial intelligence. What else should we be thinking about? Email me, opens new tab... [6745 chars]

articles.title: Apple bites OpenAI with lawsuit
articles.description: Apple has accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets as it seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT, a major rupture in a partnership between the iPhone maker and the artificial intelligence company.
articles.content:
Apple has accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets as it seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT, a major rupture in a partnership between the iPhone maker and the artificial intelligence company.

articles.description: Until last week, U.S. investors keen to jump on the memory bandwagon had few good options. Micron (NASDAQ:MU) was listed on the Nasdaq, while SK...
articles.content: Until last week, U.S. investors keen to jump on the memory bandwagon had few good options. Micron (NASDAQ:MU) was listed on the Nasdaq, while SK hynix (NASDAQ:SKHY) and Samsung were accessible only through thinly traded OTC shares or diversified Kore... [2524 chars]

articles.title: Machine learning calibration of biosensors for microcystin toxin monitoring in freshwater
articles.description: Portable screen-printed carbon electrode (SPCE) biosensors offer a rapid, low-cost way to detect microcystin-lysine-arginine (MC-LR), an extremely potent toxin produced by cyanobacteria during harmful algal blooms in freshwater. Even at low concentrations, MC-LR can damage the liver and has been linked to an increased risk of liver and colon cancer, and the World Health Organization has set a guideline value of 1 microgram per liter for MC-LR in drinking water.
articles.content: Portable screen-printed carbon electrode (SPCE) biosensors offer a rapid, low-cost way to detect microcystin-lysine-arginine (MC-LR), an extremely potent toxin produced by cyanobacteria during harmful algal blooms in freshwater. Even at low concentra... [3248 chars]

articles.description: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

articles.title: Singapore proposes mandatory notification when firms use personal data for AI training
articles.description: Organisations should issue AI-specific notifications explaining that data will be used to develop generative AI models. Read more at straitstimes.com.
articles.content:
SINGAPORE – Organisations that use personal data to train generative artificial intelligence models must clearly notify users affected under proposed new rules.
